course.title: HTML & CSS Fundamentals
course.description: Learn the building blocks of the web: HTML for structure and CSS for styling.

chapter.title: Advanced Layouts: Flexbox
chapter.description: Learn how to use CSS Flexbox to build modern, responsive layouts.

lessons.title: Introduction to Flexbox
objectives: Understand the flex container and items
lessons.explanation: The Flexible Box Layout Module (Flexbox) makes it easier to design flexible responsive layout structure without using float or positioning. To start using the Flexbox model, you need to first define a flex container using `display: flex;`.

exercises.statement: Turn the `.box-container` into a flex container.
exercises.starter_code:
Item 1
Item 2
exercises.hint: Add display: flex; to the .box-container rule.
exercises.solution:
Item 1
Item 2

lessons.title: Justify Content
lessons.explanation: The `justify-content` property is used to align the flex items along the main axis (horizontally by default). Common values are `center`, `space-between`, and `space-around`.

exercises.statement: Use `justify-content` to center the items inside the `.navbar`.
exercises.starter_code:
Home
About
exercises.hint: Add justify-content: center; to the .navbar rule.
exercises.solution:
Home
About
